description: Draws a 2D arc on a graph defined by a center point, radius, and optional angle range in degrees.
utility: Creates circular arcs with customizable center, radius, angle range, and styling

arguments.name: center
arguments.description: Center point of the arc — a point variable or inline point() expression

arguments.name: radius
arguments.description: Arc radius wrapped in radius(), e.g. radius(4)

arguments.name: range
arguments.description: Angle range in degrees, e.g. range(0, 90). Supports variable expressions. Default: full circle range(0, 360)

alternates.description: Full 360-degree arc — equivalent to a circle
Arc centered at point O with variable angle range

alternates.syntax: arc(G, O, radius(4), range(0, 120), c(orange), s(4))
alternates.description: Arc with color and stroke width

examples.scenario: Highlight minor arc between two angle-positioned points on a circle
examples.expression: O = point(G, 0, 0); circ = circle(G, O, radius(4)); A = anglepoint(G, circ, 30); B = anglepoint(G, circ, 150); arc_AB = arc(G, O, radius(4), range(30, 150), c(orange), s(4))
varanim_a = variableanimator(at(1, 30), "a", 20, 80); varanim_b = variableanimator(at(2, 30), "b", 120, 200); O = point(G, 0, 0); arc_1 = arc(G, O, radius(4), range(a, b), c(orange), s(4))
G = g2d(at(3, 1), size(20), range(-6, 6), type(geometry)); O = point(G, 0, 0); arc_1 = arc(G, O, radius(5), range(0, 90), c(red))

examples.scenario: Semicircle arc with inline center point
examples.expression: G = g2d(at(3, 1), size(20), range(-6, 6), type(geometry)); arc_1 = arc(G, point(G, 0, 0), radius(4), range(0, 180), c(blue), s(3))

notes: For angle marking (showing an angle at a vertex between two rays), prefer angle() instead of arc(). angle() supports arrowhead(), arcsize(), and angle types (interior, exterior, reflex, right). Use arc() only for geometric circular arcs (minor/major arcs on circles, semicircles, etc.).
